title: ChargePoint's Path to Profitability: Operational Gains and Strategic Alliances
text: ChargePoint reduced total cash usage to $43 million for fiscal year 2026, down dramatically from $133 million in fiscal 2025, representing a 68% reduction in cash burn and demonstrating improved working capital management and operational efficiency. More recently, ChargePoint reduced cash burn to under $2 million per quarter while maintaining $194.5 million in cash reserves. Cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash totaled $142 million as of January 31, 2026, down from $225 million a year earlier. This provides ChargePoint with sufficient runway to continue executing its strategic initiatives while working toward positive cash flow. ChargePoint reported a non-GAAP gross margin of 33%, a record since its public listing, and a GAAP gross margin of 31%, both reflecting 7-9 percentage point improvement year-over-year. The adjusted EBITDA loss improved to negative $22 million or 22% of revenue in Q2 FY26, compared to negative $34 million or 31% of revenue in Q2 FY25, representing significant year-over-year improvement. However, the path to profitability remains long and uncertain, with the company still facing significant operational challenges despite recent improvements.
